sourceText: 11th Century
translatedText: Karni na 11

sourceText: Michael Psellos
translatedText: Michael Psellos ne adam wata

sourceText: Constantly navigating the treacherous currents of court politics to maintain his position and, at times, his life.
translatedText: Ci gaba da yawo a cikin mayaudarin siyasar kotu don kiyaye matsayinsa da kuma, a wasu lokuta, rayuwarsa.

sourceText: He overcame political perils through his diplomatic skill, intellectual prowess, and by making himself an indispensable advisor to successive rulers.
translatedText: Ya shawo kan hadurran siyasa ta hanyar fasaharsa ta diflomasiyya, bajintar tunani, da kuma sanya kansa a matsayin mai ba da shawara ga masu rike da madafun iko.

sourceText: 11th Century Byzantine Empire
translatedText: Daular Byzantine na Karni na 11

sourceText: Mikhail Lomonosov
translatedText: Mikhail Lomonosov

sourceText: Humble origins and constant opposition from foreign-dominated academic circles.
translatedText: Asalin ƙasƙantar da kai da adawa akai-akai daga da'irar ilimi na ƙasashen waje.

sourceText: Through sheer perseverance, intellectual brilliance, and the patronage of enlightened figures.
translatedText: Ta hanyar jajircewa, hazaka na hankali, da taimakon hazikan mutane.

sourceText: 18th Century
translatedText: Karni na 18

sourceText: Mykhailo Drahomanov
translatedText: Mykhailo Drahomanov

sourceText: Dismissed from my university post and forced into exile by the Tsarist regime for my political views and support for Ukrainian culture.
translatedText: An kore ni daga mukamina na jami’a kuma gwamnatin Tsarist ta tilasta ni gudun hijira saboda ra’ayina na siyasa da goyon bayan al’adun Yukren.

sourceText: In exile, I founded the journal 'Hromada' in Geneva, creating a hub for Ukrainian political thought and continuing to publish works that influenced generations.
translatedText: A zaman gudun hijira, na kafa mujallar 'Hromada' a Geneva, na samar da wata kafa ta tunanin siyasar Ukraine da kuma ci gaba da buga ayyukan da suka shafi tsararraki.

sourceText: 19th Century
translatedText: Karni na 19

sourceText: Mykhailo Hrushevsky
translatedText: Mykhailo Hrushevsky

sourceText: Fighting for Ukrainian identity and independence under the oppression of both Tsarist Russia and the Soviet Union, facing political exile, arrest, and the ultimate failure of his political project.
translatedText: Yaki don tabbatar da asalin Ukrainian da 'yancin kai a karkashin zalunci na Tsarist Rasha da Tarayyar Soviet, suna fuskantar gudun hijirar siyasa, kamawa, da gazawar karshe na aikin siyasarsa.

sourceText: He channeled his efforts into his magnum opus, the 'History of Ukraine-Rus'', to establish a distinct national narrative, and briefly realized his political vision as the head of the Central Rada, laying the groundwork for Ukrainian statehood.
translatedText: Ya gabatar da kokarinsa a cikin babban opus dinsa, 'Tarihin Ukraine-Rus'', don kafa labari na musamman na kasa, kuma a takaice ya tabbatar da hangen nesansa na siyasa a matsayinsa na shugaban Rada ta Tsakiya, yana shimfida harsashi ga kasar Yukren.

sourceText: Late 19th - Early 20th Century
translatedText: Marigayi 19th - Farkon ƙarni na 20

sourceText: The repeated loss of his ancestral homeland, Samarkand, and the years spent as a landless prince in exile.
translatedText: Yawan asarar ƙasar kakanninsa, Samarkand, da kuma shekarun da aka yi a matsayin basarake mara ƙasa a gudun hijira.

sourceText: By turning his ambitions towards India, he conquered Delhi and established the foundations of the Mughal Empire, forging a new destiny.
translatedText: Ta hanyar juya burinsa zuwa Indiya, ya ci Delhi kuma ya kafa harsashin daular Mughal, ya kafa sabuwar makoma.

sourceText: 15th-16th Century
translatedText: Karni na 15-16

sourceText: Internal rebellions and constant external threats
translatedText: Tawayen cikin gida da barazanar waje akai-akai

sourceText: Military genius, ruthless determination, and strategic alliances
translatedText: Hazakar soja, azama mara tausayi, da kawancen dabaru

sourceText: Late 10th - Early 11th Century
translatedText: Marigayi 10th - Farkon Ƙarni na 11

sourceText: Witnessing the lack of complete monastic rules (Vinaya) in China and enduring the loss of companions during his treacherous journey.
translatedText: Shaidawa rashin cikakkun ka'idojin zuhudu (Vinaya) a kasar Sin da jure rashin sahabbai yayin tafiyarsa ta ha'inci.

sourceText: His unwavering faith and singular focus on his mission to acquire the sacred texts allowed him to persevere through immense hardship and return to China.
translatedText: Bangaskiyarsa marar kaskantar da kai da mayar da hankali ga manufarsa na samun littattafai masu tsarki sun ba shi damar dagewa ta wurin wahala mai yawa kuma ya koma kasar Sin.

sourceText: 4th-5th Century CE
translatedText: Karni na 4-5 CE

sourceText: The constant suspicion of the Emperor and the political intrigues of the court.
translatedText: Zargin da akai na Sarkin sarakuna da makircin siyasa na kotu.

sourceText: Through unwavering loyalty to the Empire and proving my worth with victories on the battlefield.
translatedText: Ta hanyar biyayya ga Daular da kuma tabbatar da kimara tare da nasarori a fagen fama.

sourceText: 6th Century
translatedText: Karni na 6

sourceText: Bohdan Khmelnytsky
translatedText: Bohdan Khmelnytsky

sourceText: Oppression by Polish magnates, personal tragedy, and the betrayal of allies.
translatedText: Zaluntar manyan 'yan Poland, bala'i na sirri, da cin amanar abokan tarayya.

sourceText: Uniting the Cossacks for a massive rebellion and establishing the Cossack Hetmanate through military and diplomatic means.
translatedText: Haɗin kan Cossack don gagarumin tawaye da kafa Cossack Hetmanate ta hanyar soja da diflomasiyya.

sourceText: 17th Century
translatedText: Karni na 17

sourceText: Bumin Qaghan
translatedText: Bumin Qagan

sourceText: Enduring subjugation under the Rouran Khaganate and suffering the ultimate humiliation when its ruler refused my marriage proposal, calling me his 'blacksmith slave'.
translatedText: Darewa da jure wa zalunci a ƙarƙashin Rouran Khaganate da shan azabar wulakanci na ƙarshe lokacin da mai mulkinsa ya ƙi neman aure na, yana kirana da 'bawan maƙeri'.

sourceText: By uniting the Turkic tribes, forging a strategic alliance with the Western Wei dynasty, and decisively defeating the Rouran, I established my own Khaganate and claimed my rightful place.
translatedText: Ta hanyar haɗa ƙabilun Turkawa, da kulla ƙawance mai mahimmanci tare da daular Wei ta Yamma, da fatattakar Ruran, na kafa nawa Khaganate kuma na ɗauki wurin da ya dace.

sourceText: 6th Century
translatedText: Karni na 6

sourceText: A brutal power struggle against my half-brother Yaropolk and the immense challenge of uniting disparate, pagan tribes.
translatedText: Mummunan gwagwarmayar mulki da ɗan'uwana Yaropolk da babban ƙalubale na haɗa ƙabilun arna.

sourceText: Through military campaigns, strategic alliances, and ultimately the adoption of a single state religion, I forged a unified Kievan Rus' with a shared identity.
translatedText: Ta hanyar yaƙin neman zaɓe na soji, ƙawance na dabaru, da kuma ɗaukar nauyin addini ɗaya na jiha, na ƙirƙiri haɗin kai Kievan Rus' tare da ainihin asali.

sourceText: Late 10th - Early 11th Century
translatedText: Marigayi 10th - Farkon Ƙarni na 11

sourceText: Vladimir Vernadsky
translatedText: Vladimir Vernadsky

sourceText: He had to continue his research amidst the chaos of the Russian Revolution and Civil War, facing political pressures that threatened his scientific freedom.
translatedText: Dole ne ya ci gaba da bincikensa a cikin rudani na juyin juya halin Rasha da yakin basasa, yana fuskantar matsin lamba na siyasa da ke barazana ga 'yancin kimiyya.

sourceText: Through his unwavering commitment to science and the support of his colleagues, he founded the Ukrainian Academy of Sciences and continued his research, developing his theories on the biosphere despite the turbulent times.
translatedText: Ta hanyar sadaukar da kai ga kimiyya da goyon bayan abokan aikinsa, ya kafa Cibiyar Kimiyya ta Ukrainian kuma ya ci gaba da bincikensa, yana haɓaka ka'idodinsa akan biosphere duk da rikice-rikice.

sourceText: 19th-20th Century
translatedText: Karni na 19-20

sourceText: Sergei Korolev
translatedText: Sergey Korolev

sourceText: Accused as an 'enemy of the people' during Stalin's Great Purge, I endured years of brutal imprisonment in a Gulag.
translatedText: An zarge ni a matsayin 'maƙiyin mutane' a lokacin Babban Tsarkakewar Stalin, na jimre tsawon shekaru na ɗaurin kurkuku a Gulag.

sourceText: After my release, I became the Chief Designer of the Soviet space program, channeling my experiences into an unyielding drive for success.
translatedText: Bayan an sake ni, na zama Babban Mai tsara shirin sararin samaniyar Soviet, inda na ba da abubuwan da na gani a cikin yunƙurin nasara.

sourceText: 20th Century Space Race
translatedText: tseren sararin samaniya na ƙarni na 20

sourceText: Semyon Dezhnev
translatedText: Semyon Dezhnev

sourceText: The brutal Siberian wilderness, constant danger, loss of men and ships, and the fact his monumental discovery went unrecognized for nearly a century.
translatedText: Mummunan jejin Siberiya, haɗari na yau da kullun, asarar maza da jiragen ruwa, da kuma gaskiyar bincikensa mai girma ba a san shi ba kusan kusan ƙarni guda.

sourceText: Through sheer resilience, survival skills, and a steadfast commitment to his duties, he endured. His legacy was secured when his detailed reports were finally rediscovered in the archives.
translatedText: Ta hanyar juriya, basirar rayuwa, da jajircewa kan ayyukansa, ya jure. An sami nasarar gadonsa lokacin da aka sake gano cikakkun rahotannin nasa a cikin ma'ajiyar bayanai.

sourceText: 17th Century
translatedText: Karni na 17

sourceText: Shota Rustaveli
translatedText: Shota Rustaveli

sourceText: The scarcity of historical records about my life, leaving my personal story shrouded in mystery.
translatedText: Karancin bayanan tarihi game da rayuwata, ya bar labarina na sirri rufa-rufa.

sourceText: Achieving immortality through my work, as my epic poem has preserved my philosophy and spirit for centuries.
translatedText: Samun dawwama ta wurin aikina, kamar yadda waƙar waƙa ta ke kiyaye falsafa da ruhina tsawon ƙarni.

sourceText: 12th Century Georgian Golden Age
translatedText: Zamanin Zinare na Georgian na Karni na 12

sourceText: Leo the Mathematician
translatedText: Leo Masanin Lissafi

sourceText: His academic pursuits were initially stifled by the political turmoil of the Iconoclast controversy, and he faced jealousy and opposition.
translatedText: Da farko dai hargitsin siyasa na rigimar Iconoclast ta hana shi neman ilimi, kuma ya fuskanci kishi da adawa.

sourceText: He gained the patronage of the powerful Bardas, who appointed him to re-found and lead the Magnaura School, making it a center of learning.
translatedText: Ya sami goyon bayan Bardas mai iko, wanda ya nada shi don sake kafa makarantar Magnaura, ya mai da ita cibiyar ilmantarwa.

sourceText: 9th Century Byzantine Empire
translatedText: Daular Byzantine na Karni na 9

sourceText: Abai Qunanbaiuly
translatedText: Abai Qunanbaiuly

sourceText: Struggling against the oppressive policies of the Russian Empire and the rigid, outdated feudal traditions of his own society.
translatedText: Gwagwarmaya da azzaluman manufofin daular Rasha da kuma tsattsauran ra'ayi, tsoffin al'adun feudal na al'ummarsa.

sourceText: He forged a new path by enlightening his people through poetry, philosophical works, and translations, creating a modern Kazakh cultural identity.
translatedText: Ya kirkiro wata sabuwar hanya ta hanyar fadakar da mutanensa ta hanyar wakoki, ayyukan falsafa, da fassarorin, samar da asalin al'adun Kazakh na zamani.

sourceText: 19th Century
translatedText: Karni na 19

sourceText: Persecution, exile to Siberia, imprisonment, and the martyrdom of his family and followers for opposing church reforms.
translatedText: Tsananta, gudun hijira zuwa Siberiya, ɗaurin kurkuku, da kuma shahadar iyalinsa da mabiyansa don adawa da sake fasalin coci.

sourceText: Found strength in unyielding faith, writing his influential autobiography, and becoming a martyr-saint for the Old Believers.
translatedText: Ya sami ƙarfi cikin bangaskiya mara jurewa, rubuta tarihin rayuwar sa mai tasiri, da zama shahidi- waliyyi ga Tsofaffin Muminai.

sourceText: 17th Century
translatedText: Karni na 17

sourceText: Attila the Hun
translatedText: Attila da Hun

sourceText: The internal fragmentation of his empire, constant resistance from external powers, and the relentless pressure of continuous warfare to maintain control.
translatedText: Rugujewar daularsa ta cikin gida, da tsayin daka daga iko na waje, da matsi na ci gaba da yaki don kiyaye iko.

sourceText: Maintained imperial control by crushing rebellions and subjugating enemies with overwhelming military force and ruthless tactics.
translatedText: Ci gaba da kula da daular ta hanyar murkushe tawaye da murkushe abokan gaba da karfin soji da dabarun rashin tausayi.

sourceText: 5th Century
translatedText: Karni na 5

sourceText: Ahmad Yasawi
translatedText: Ahmad Yasawi

sourceText: The internal struggle between worldly knowledge and spiritual enlightenment, and the challenge of making the essence of Islam accessible to the common people.
translatedText: Gwagwarmayar cikin gida tsakanin ilimin duniya da wayewar ruhi, da kalubalen samar da ainihin Musulunci ga jama'a.

sourceText: He channeled his spiritual insights into poetry, composing in the common Turkic language to make the profound concepts of Sufism understandable and relatable to all.
translatedText: Ya ba da ra'ayoyinsa na ruhaniya a cikin waƙa, yana tsara cikin harshen Turkawa na gama gari don fahimtar fahimtar Sufanci mai zurfi kuma ya dace da kowa.

sourceText: 12th Century
translatedText: Karni na 12

sourceText: Anna Komnene
translatedText: Anna Komnene

sourceText: Sidelined from the imperial succession and implicated in a plot against her brother, she was stripped of her property and exiled to a monastery.
translatedText: An kebe ta daga gadon sarauta kuma aka sanya ta cikin wani makirci da aka yi wa dan uwanta, an kwace mata kadarorinta kuma aka kai ta gudun hijira zuwa gidan zuhudu.

sourceText: In exile, she channeled her grief into scholarship, composing her magnum opus, 'The Alexiad,' to immortalize her father's reign.
translatedText: A zaman gudun hijira, ta mayar da bakin cikinta zuwa guraben karatu, inda ta hada babban littafinta, 'The Alexiad,' don ta dawwamar da mulkin mahaifinta.

sourceText: 11th-12th Century Byzantine Empire
translatedText: Daular Byzantine ta Karni na 11-12

sourceText: Andrei Rublev
translatedText: Andrei Rublev

sourceText: The constant violence and instability of the Tatar Yoke, and the inner struggle between artistic vision and monastic humility.
translatedText: Rikici na yau da kullun da rashin kwanciyar hankali na Tatar Yoke, da gwagwarmayar ciki tsakanin hangen nesa na fasaha da tawali'u na zuhudu.

sourceText: Finding solace and purpose in prayer, meditation, and the creation of art that sought to reveal divine harmony and peace to a troubled world.
translatedText: Neman ta'aziyya da manufa cikin addu'a, tunani, da ƙirƙirar fasaha waɗanda suka nemi bayyana jituwa da salama na Allah ga duniya mai wahala.

sourceText: 14th-15th Century
translatedText: Karni na 14-15

sourceText: Alexander Nevsky
translatedText: Alexander Nevsky

sourceText: Navigating the immense pressure of protecting his realm from two fronts: the Catholic Crusaders from the West and the powerful Mongol Empire (the Golden Horde) from the East.
translatedText: Kewaya babban matsin lamba na kare mulkinsa daga bangarori biyu: 'Yan Salibiyya Katolika daga Yamma da Daular Mongol mai karfi (Golden Horde) daga Gabas.

sourceText: Employing a dual policy: decisively defeating Western invaders militarily while pragmatically submitting to the Mongol khans to preserve the Russian lands, people, and Orthodox faith.
translatedText: Yin amfani da manufofin dual: fatattakar mamaya na Yamma da ƙarfi ta hanyar soja yayin da suke mika wuya ga Mongol khans don kiyaye ƙasashen Rasha, mutane, da bangaskiyar Orthodox.

sourceText: 13th Century
translatedText: Karni na 13

sourceText: Alexander Pushkin
translatedText: Alexander Pushkin

sourceText: Exile and strict censorship by the Tsar for my liberal political views, and constant social pressures that ultimately led to my death.
translatedText: Yin gudun hijira da tsattsauran ra'ayi daga Tsar don ra'ayina na siyasa mai sassaucin ra'ayi, da matsin lamba na zamantakewa wanda a ƙarshe ya kai ga mutuwa.

sourceText: I channeled my isolation and frustrations into my most creative periods, using folklore and history to craft a new, purely Russian literary voice.
translatedText: Na keɓance keɓance kaina da bacin rai a cikin mafi kyawun lokaci na, ta yin amfani da labarun almara da tarihi don kera sabuwar muryar adabin Rasha zalla.

sourceText: 19th Century
translatedText: Karni na 19

sourceText: Alexios I Komnenos
translatedText: Alexios I Komnenos

sourceText: Inheriting a collapsing empire besieged by Normans, Pechenegs, and Seljuk Turks.
translatedText: Gaji daular rugujewa da Normans, Pechenegs, da Seljuk Turks suka yiwa kawanya.

sourceText: Through shrewd diplomacy, military reforms, and leveraging the First Crusade, I stabilized the empire and began its recovery.
translatedText: Ta hanyar diflomasiyya mai wayo, gyare-gyaren soja, da yin amfani da yakin Crusade na farko, na daidaita daular kuma na fara farfadowa.

sourceText: 11th-12th Century
translatedText: Karni na 11-12

sourceText: Alisher Navoi
translatedText: Alisher Navoi

sourceText: The struggle to prove the literary value of Turkic languages in an environment dominated by the prestige of Persian literature.
translatedText: Gwagwarmayar tabbatar da darajar adabi na harsunan Turkawa a muhallin da martabar adabin Farisa ya mamaye.

sourceText: By writing masterpieces and treatises like 'The Comparison of the Two Languages', he demonstrated the richness of Turkic and established Chagatai as a respected literary language.
translatedText: Ta hanyar rubuta manyan zane-zane da rubuce-rubuce kamar 'Kwantancin Harsuna Biyu', ya nuna wadatar Turkawa kuma ya kafa Chagatai a matsayin harshen adabi mai mutuntawa.

sourceText: Timurid Empire, 15th Century
translatedText: Daular Timurid, Karni na 15

sourceText: Accused of heresy and faced political persecution, leading to his exile from major cities like Nishapur and his hometown of Bukhara.
translatedText: An zarge shi da bidi'a kuma ya fuskanci zalunci na siyasa, wanda ya kai ga gudun hijira daga manyan garuruwa kamar Nishapur da garinsa na Bukhara.

sourceText: He remained steadfast in his scholarly principles and dedicated himself to teaching and completing his life's work, which ultimately became his enduring legacy.
translatedText: Ya tsaya tsayin daka kan ka'idodinsa na ilimi kuma ya sadaukar da kansa ga koyarwa da kammala aikinsa na rayuwarsa, wanda a ƙarshe ya zama gadonsa na dindindin.

sourceText: 9th Century / Abbasid Caliphate
translatedText: Karni na 9 / Khalifancin Abbasid

sourceText: Al-Farghani
translatedText: Al-Farghani

sourceText: The challenge of simplifying the complex astronomical theories of the ancient Greeks for a wider audience.
translatedText: Kalubalen sauƙaƙa rikitattun ka'idodin falaki na tsohuwar Helenawa don yawan masu sauraro.

sourceText: By writing the 'Elements of Astronomy,' a concise and systematic summary of Ptolemy's Almagest.
translatedText: Ta hanyar rubuta 'Elements of Astronomy,' taƙaitaccen taƙaitaccen bayani na Ptolemy's Almagest.

sourceText: 9th Century
translatedText: Karni na 9

sourceText: If you live in hatred, in quarrels and fighting, you will perish yourselves and ruin the land of your fathers and grandfathers, which they had created with their great labor.
translatedText: Idan kuna rayuwa cikin ƙiyayya, da husuma, da faɗa, za ku hallaka kanku, ku lalatar da ƙasar kakanninku da kakanninku, wadda suka halitta da babban aikinsu.

sourceText: Bloody succession wars against my brothers and constant threats from nomadic tribes like the Pechenegs.
translatedText: Yaƙe-yaƙe na gadon jini da ƴan uwana da kuma barazanar ƙabilun makiyaya kamar Pechenegs.

sourceText: I secured my throne through military victory and shrewd diplomacy, then focused on internal consolidation through law, education, and strategic alliances.
translatedText: Na sami gadon sarauta ta ta hanyar nasarar soja da diflomasiyya mai wayo, sannan na mai da hankali kan karfafa cikin gida ta hanyar doka, ilimi, da kawancen dabaru.

sourceText: 11th Century
translatedText: Karni na 11

sourceText: Yermak Timofeyevich
translatedText: Yermak Timofeyevich

sourceText: Facing a vast, unknown wilderness and a numerically superior enemy with only a small band of men.
translatedText: Fuskantar babban jeji, wanda ba a san shi ba da kuma maƙiyi mafi girman ƙima tare da ƴan ƙaramin rukuni na maza.

sourceText: My campaign, though it cost me my life, opened Siberia for Russia, creating an empire stretching to the Pacific.
translatedText: Yaƙin neman zaɓe na, ko da yake ya kashe ni rayuwata, ya buɗe Siberiya ga Rasha, ya haifar da daula da ta miƙe zuwa Pacific.

sourceText: 16th Century
translatedText: Karni na 16

sourceText: John Chrysostom
translatedText: John Chrysostom

sourceText: Conflict with the imperial court, leading to two exiles and a harsh life away from his flock.
translatedText: Rikici da kotun daular, wanda ya kai ga gudun hijira biyu da rayuwa mai tsauri daga garken sa.

sourceText: Maintained his faith and influence through extensive letter-writing and theological work, even during his exile.
translatedText: Ya kiyaye bangaskiyarsa da tasirinsa ta wurin rubutaccen wasiƙa da aikin tauhidi, har ma a lokacin gudun hijira.

sourceText: Late 4th Century Roman Empire
translatedText: Marigayi Daular Rum ta Ƙarni na 4

sourceText: The constant struggle between his duties as a ruler and his passion for science, culminating in a rebellion led by his own son.
translatedText: Gwagwarmayar da aka saba yi tsakanin ayyukansa na mai mulki da sha'awar kimiyya, ta kai ga tawaye da ɗansa ya jagoranta.

sourceText: Though his political career ended in tragedy, his scientific legacy, particularly the 'Zij-i Sultani' star catalogue, influenced astronomy for centuries.
translatedText: Duk da cewa aikinsa na siyasa ya ƙare cikin bala'i, abin da ya gada a kimiyyance, musamman kasida ta tauraron 'Zij-i Sultani', ya yi tasiri ga ilimin taurari tsawon ƙarni.

sourceText: 15th Century Timurid Empire
translatedText: Karni na 15 Timurid Empire

sourceText: Justinian I
translatedText: Justinian I

sourceText: The devastating Nika riots nearly cost me my throne and burned much of Constantinople. Later, a terrible plague swept through the empire, decimating the population and weakening our strength.
translatedText: Mummunan tarzomar Nika ta kusan kashe ni a gadon sarautata kuma ta kona yawancin Konstantinoful. Daga baya, wata mummunar annoba ta mamaye daular, ta rage yawan jama’a kuma ta raunana ƙarfinmu.

sourceText: With the counsel of my wife Theodora, I crushed the revolt. I then focused on rebuilding, codifying the law to create order, and commissioning the magnificent Hagia Sophia as a testament to God's glory and my power.
translatedText: Da shawarar matata Theodora, na murkushe tawayen. Daga nan na mayar da hankali wajen sake ginawa, tsara doka don samar da oda, da kuma nada fitacciyar Hajiya Sophia a matsayin shaida ga daukakar Allah da kuma ikona.

sourceText: 6th Century
translatedText: Karni na 6

sourceText: Ivan III (the Great)
translatedText: Ivan III (mai girma)

sourceText: We, by the grace of God, have been sovereigns in our land from the beginning, from our first forebears.
translatedText: Mu da yardar Allah mun kasance masu mulki a kasarmu tun daga farko tun daga kakanninmu na farko.

sourceText: Ruling amidst constant threats from the Golden Horde, the Grand Duchy of Lithuania, and internal rivalries among Rus' princes.
translatedText: Hukunce-hukuncen ci gaba da barazana daga Golden Horde, Grand Duchy na Lithuania, da fafatawa a tsakanin sarakunan Rus.

sourceText: Through shrewd diplomacy, strategic military campaigns, and a relentless drive to centralize power, I unified the Russian lands and asserted our sovereignty.
translatedText: Ta hanyar diflomasiyya mai wayo, yaƙin neman zaɓe na soja, da yunƙurin mayar da mulki ba tare da ɓata lokaci ba, na haɗa ƙasashen Rasha kuma na tabbatar da ikonmu.

sourceText: 15th Century
translatedText: Karni na 15

sourceText: Isolation and betrayal by the boyars during his childhood, and the death of his beloved wife, Anastasia.
translatedText: Warewa da cin amana ta boyars a lokacin ƙuruciyarsa, da mutuwar ƙaunataccen matarsa, Anastasia.

sourceText: Seized absolute power, crushed the boyars, and centralized the state, expanding Russia into an empire.
translatedText: Kame cikakken iko, ya murkushe boyars, kuma ya daidaita jihar, ya fadada Rasha zuwa daular.

sourceText: 16th Century
translatedText: Karni na 16

sourceText: Ivan Mazepa
translatedText: Ivan Mazepa

sourceText: The crushing defeat at Poltava, which shattered my dream of an independent Ukraine and led to my being branded a traitor by the Russian Empire.
translatedText: Mugun shan kayen da aka yi a Poltava, wanda ya tarwatsa mafarkina na samun 'yancin kai na Ukraine kuma ya sa daular Rasha ta yi min lakabi da maci amana.

sourceText: Though I died in exile, my actions inspired generations of Ukrainians to fight for their sovereignty. My story became a powerful symbol of national resistance, immortalized in art and literature across Europe.
translatedText: Ko da yake na mutu a zaman gudun hijira, abin da na yi ya ƙarfafa tsararraki na ’yan Ukrain su yi yaƙi don ’yancin kansu. Labarina ya zama alama mai ƙarfi na juriya na ƙasa, dawwama cikin fasaha da adabi a faɗin Turai.

sourceText: Late 17th - Early 18th Century
translatedText: Marigayi 17th - Farkon ƙarni na 18

sourceText: Ivan Franko
translatedText: Ivan Franko

sourceText: Our goal is human happiness, freedom, and brotherhood.
translatedText: Burinmu shine farin cikin ɗan adam, 'yanci, da 'yan uwantaka.

sourceText: Political oppression by the Austro-Hungarian Empire, repeated imprisonment for socialist activities, and being ostracized from academia.
translatedText: Zaluntar siyasa daga daular Austro-Hungary, daurin dauri akai-akai saboda ayyukan gurguzu, da kuma keɓe shi daga ilimin kimiyya.

sourceText: He channeled his struggles into a prolific output of literature, scholarship, and political writing, awakening the national consciousness and spirit of the Ukrainian people.
translatedText: Ya ba da gwagwarmayar gwagwarmayar da ya yi a cikin ɗimbin ɗimbin wallafe-wallafe, malanta, da rubuce-rubucen siyasa, yana farkar da wayewar ƙasa da ruhin al'ummar Ukrainian.

sourceText: Late 19th - Early 20th Century
translatedText: Marigayi 19th - Farkon ƙarni na 20

sourceText: Ismail Gasprinsky
translatedText: Ismail Gasprinsky

sourceText: Facing oppression from the Russian Empire and resistance from conservative elements within Muslim society.
translatedText: Fuskantar zalunci daga daular Rasha da tsayin daka daga masu ra'ayin mazan jiya a cikin al'ummar musulmi.

sourceText: Championed modernization and unity through his newspaper Terciman and the 'New Method' (Usul-i Jadid) of education.
translatedText: Ya sami nasarar zamanantar da haɗin kai ta hanyar jaridarsa Terciman da 'Sabuwar Hanyar' (Usul-i Jadid) na ilimi.

sourceText: Late 19th - Early 20th Century
translatedText: Marigayi 19th - Farkon ƙarni na 20

sourceText: Jamshid al-Kashi
translatedText: Jamshid al-Kashi

sourceText: Early in my career, I struggled with poverty and a lack of patronage for my scientific pursuits.
translatedText: A farkon sana’ata, na yi fama da talauci da kuma rashin samun goyon bayan ayyukan kimiyya.

sourceText: The invitation from Prince Ulugh Beg to join his new observatory in Samarkand provided me with the resources and stability to dedicate my life to science.
translatedText: Gayyatar da Yarima Ulugh Beg ya yi na shiga sabon dakin bincikensa a Samarkand ya ba ni albarkatu da kwanciyar hankali don sadaukar da rayuwata ga kimiyya.

sourceText: 15th Century Timurid Empire
translatedText: Karni na 15 Timurid Empire
